foresight_scores.hypothesis: pluggy is the bottommost dependency of the entire pytest/tox/devpi ecosystem, so it deliberately maintains zero runtime dependencies — because a single dependency here propagates to the whole ecosystem

Performance of the hook-call hot path is an explicit design priority — since pytest calls hooks tens of thousands of times, the tradeoff of raising registration (cold) cost to lower call (hot) cost is repeated

Maintaining backward compatibility dictates even architectural decisions — keeping a separate resident function for the old-style hookwrapper executor, and even maintaining a suppress list waiting for third-party fix releases, reflects the principle that 'the library bears the cost until the ecosystem migrates'
